title: linaria arvensis subsp. micrantha
intro: Linaria arvensis subsp. micrantha, also known as small-toothed toadflax, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, and is often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Linaria arvensis subsp. micrantha can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to soothe sore throats and calm digestive issues.
